name: Abzu
potentialConcerns: none. Unless its fear of water
summary: Abzu is a genuinely beautiful game. The pleayer plays a robot diver, who wakes up in a incredibly vibrant and beautiful ocean. The player works to restore life towards the ocean as theu explore ancient ruins and look to shutdown amchines that are harvesting the ocean's life energy. Guided by a spiritual-like great white shark, the player sets off to fix the ocean and restore all of its beauty.
details:
Peace and ease: it is like not easy to say in one aspect for this game since it really does everything you oculd imagine. It is great for children who are cognitivally impaired as it is not a complex game, with an extremely low stakes and slow pacing that allows for players to really take it on their own time. the beauty of the game comes from seeing just how beautiful the ocean is. It gives the players a really good calm and allows for players to just sit back and enjoy the game for its amazing graphics and ease. 
Environmental care, as well as mental health: This game really does everything im my opinion. It is so beautiful and teaches to care for the world around the player as you are actively trying to restore the ocean to what it once was. The player learns to care for each animal that returns and follows along throughout the journey as well as overall cleanliness and peace
Sensory, Calm, Anti-stress: The vibrant beautiful colors of the game are the most prime example imaginable for a game that allows the player to just watch a game. It is incredibly good for kids who can be over stimulated as well troubled by games that are too violent or creepy. It really does it all

name: Animal Crossing: New horizons
playersOnline: Solo, however, multiple people are allowed to play on one consecutive "Island" although not at the same time. Also some multiplayer capacity, but very limited. Dependent on inviting people online, so will not have random people interacting
potentialConcerns: no worries for strangers online as specifically invited people that are friended through the Switch's device are the only people allowed to interact.
summary: you are put on a tropical island in which you have different animal villagers that you can interact with. You are able to decorate your home and island to however you desire, as well as earn money through selling resources that you find such as fruits, flowers, and even fish as well. ACNH allows people to live a social simulation game that lets them interact with cute villagers as well as  develop their own sense for what they want to do in their own world
details: Interaction with villageras and NPCs in general allows SOCIAL INTERACTION with interactive dialogue and an emotions based system in which the chracters will respond accordingly to the kindness that the player exhibits. This can specifically target sadness, as it allows for them to have dialogue with a "person" even if they are extremely reclused                                The island is meant for development, you are able to farm, raise, fish, grow, and change the  entire island according to exactly how the player wants it, so it is defintely highly recommended for patients of long term stay as it is something they are able to play and work on for a very long time as well is something that they are able to take away with themselves for memories or future play.  The fishing, money earning, cute clothes, furniture gathering,  renovations towards their own personal homes as well as entire island allows for this game to have infinite possiblities for the player to pursue without getting bored.

name: Dave the Diver
potentialConcerns: could be a bit scary/anxiety inducing in some situations
summary: Dave, is a diver who has to explore and catch fish in the Deep Blue, a mysterious ocean that changes its topography and appeareances each day. The Deep Blue features exotic fish from all over the world as well as sea monsters and sea people as well. By day, Dave catches fish on his diving journeys that he brings back at night where he operates as a worker at his sushi restaurant opened by his friends and him. Fish, sell, upgrade, and find resources to develop your diving experiences!
details:
helps with sadness and depression as Dave the diver is one of the most peaceful yet fun life simulators that can be taken both seriously and unseriously 
the game is calming due to the fact that the pixel art style, coupled with the funny animations as well as beautiful ocean come together to create a really beautiful game that.
The rewarding aspect of the game comes from the customization as well as optimization you get from operating the sushi store as well as the dives. You are able to earn money from selling your fish, and that money goes into upgrading Dave's equipment as well as making the store more customized towards your own appearences.  You are able to earn TONS of in game money and can upgrade your employees as well as open branches of the store to make even more money.
Social interaction in the game comes from interactions with the NPC (Non-plyable-characters) who are absolutely the greatest in this game. Theres weird characters, sassy characters, fun characters, serious characters and all sorts of other personalites that are incorporated in each of their designs and animations which is really unique in these types of games as it shows great uniqueness that makes each charcter extremely lovable such as bancho, the head sushi chef at your restaurant that says VERY few words, but sends all his messages trhough the food that he makes. 
The pacing of the game is completely dependent on your self, except for the aspect that you are allowed to fish, initially, twice in the day before heading back to your store to open up for each night. But within these time frames you are free to do anything to your owndesires within either material gathering or just fishing with your harpoon or nets/ other equipments. 
Very adorable game, though can be anxiety inducing due to the aspect that there are aggressive fish as well as an oxygen limit (that can be upgraed as well as oxygen deposits trhoughout the dives) that encompass as a limiting factor and timer for your explorations, but despite that I still think it can help with stress alot as the characters and overall peacefulness of the deep, ever changing exploration gives a sense of awe and ease for how exapnsive and free the game is.

name: Fire Emblem: Three Houses
potentialConcerns:
Violence - (death) with combat - comprising a medivial knight archetype story
Religious as well as  political views, despite being completey fictional, some could see the game as being sacriligious, but it is a game that has a made up religion and aspects of God and creation
summary: Fire Emblem: Three Houses is an exceptionally complex and long story that a short summary would not be able to do justice. Players play as Byleth, who becomes a professor at a monastary that exists as the central force of religion on an isolated continent called Fodlan. Fodlan is divided into three regions with each one having extremely complex religious, as well as political views. The player is given a choice to teach/guide one of the three houses that reside in the monstary, with each houes comprising of a future successor of the three politcal powerhouse regions. Eventually you go throuhgout the story to combat the land's wars and a secret cult that plots to take over the world and "destroy" the original progenitor of the world.
details:
Politics/Social balances: The main reason I put Fire Emblem here is because I believe it is a truly essential game that allows for young teens to develop and understand political views as well as actions in a story based game. The three region's political standings and views all differ from each other, and the player is able to fully explore them trhoughout their play throughs. this is NOT meant to be political in any sense, but it is able to introduce the complexities in how religion, different economic perspectives can guide a nation or destroy one. THIS DOES NOTTTTT create any modern life political views, but rather is able to create a sense of world view in which people themselves are able to determine what that they deem is right or wrong, or what they want to fulfill with their desires
Grief/Loss: There is death in this game. Players and their companions kill the "evil" people in the game though not in a gory or grotesque way at all. However there are key figures in the game that die as well as key figures that are killed which can impact players with a sense of loss or hate. This is really important to show in media for children as it allows for them to see grief and different ways to process it as well as understand the importance of life in so many aspects. Whether that be in consideration of a large nation in combat, or for each individual person. The importance of life as well as enjoying the choices it gives you is one of the most important points made in this game.

name: It Takes Two
potentialConcerns: There is potential in concern as the story centers around a couple who is having intense marital conflict - as well as there being language in it a little too. The game might be difficult relying on two people to solve puzzles as well
summary: It takes two is a co-op comedy puzzle game in which a married couple mid argument are transferred into a comic-like, miniature world within their daughter's dolls. here they have to set aside their differences and solve puzzles guided by a book of love to reconcile both their relationship and return to their original world
details:
Teamwork: It takes two requires a LOT of team effort. The game is simply impossible to play without both players working together and trying their best. It teaches good communication that has to be navigated through both puzzles and combat as well as just overall exploration throughout the game. 
Relationships: It takes two can be considered a heavy game, despite its light-hearted gameplay and appareances. However, the main story of the game is a couple mid-divorce falling back in love again and working together. It can be slightly scary for children who play it in a similar situation, but I think it really highlights how both sides in a marriage have to commit and understand each other for not just their own sake, but their family's as well. It really does a good job at teaching a deep lesson in a light hearted way, and it can also help with the pain surrounding the child as well

name: Just Dance
playersOnline: Solo but much more fun with multiplayer
potentialConcerns: Some more difficult songs could become cardiovasulary intense for some kids, but overall should be okay as fatigue should just stop them from burning out too much
summary: Just Dance puts all time popular songs from all around the world comprising of several genres, and turn them into dance simulator games. For example, if it took 24Karat by Bruno mars, It would show avatars on the screen, and holding a controller in hand, players are supposed to follow along the dance shown on screen throguhout the duration of a song. This allows for very good exercise and is able to keep kids active without taking away the fun, as well as incorporating modern and past songs that really any person can appreciate.
details:
Social: Just dance is a very social game as it really allows for several people to just get up and dance and laugh as everyone attempts to mimic the difficult dance patterns as well as just sing along with songs too. 
EXERCISE: Just dance is a fantastic way for kids to stay active especially for kids who do not want to go outside and run around. Just dance not only features an exercise mode, but really makes moving and sweating an overall very fun time. The music and dances are all entertaining enough that kids can play for endless amounts of time, but it also really allows for simplicity in that there doesnt have to be perfect dancing. As long as the movements feel right, the game is able to tell that and reward players making exercise both fun and rewarding.

name: Mario Kart 8: Deluxe & World
potentialConcerns: With intense racing games like this, potentially could get too competitive or upset over not winning a high place such as 1st place.
summary: Choose from several maps as well as several characters, vehicles, tires, gliders, and race across the world of mario maps. Throughtout the maps that you can race on, there are several hazards that are comprised from the mario game world such as chain chomps that can attack players. Beyond that, players race to gain items that can be used against each other or for themselves and compete against others to win.
details:
Social Themes: the game allows for very easy social interaction as you are able to easily cycle in several players as desired or play with only a couiple against computers. Very easy way for children to bridge the social divide by allowing them to easily and without much potential violence in a friendly racing game
Stress: Can help with stress as the fast pace action in racing allows for players to have rapid dopamine from competing and potentiall let out a lot of pent up anxiety and stress.
Simplicity: I think Mario kart is one of the few games that are simple enough for really all age ranges to understand and play, and regardless of not being able to understand the game, it really allows for people to feel involved especially for isolated children that are cognitivally impaired, there is not enough complexion that could frustrate them and allows for them to easily play the game

name: Minecraft
avatarGender: Multiple, although you start with just Steve, a base male figure as the first playable charcter
genre: Sandbox(you can do whatever you want, really.) Role-playing, survival simulation
playersOnline: Solo as well as online play available
potentialConcerns: Can be scary to the youngest of audiences when played with a certain difficulty. There are difficulty settings that can turn on or off whether monsters spawn or not.
summary: 3D sandbox game that lets you do just about anything. From exploring, crafting, resource gathering, building, combat, and farming, Minecraft lets the player choose exactly what they want to do. The linear story features gathering enough of certain resources to be able to travel to other dimensions to defeat certain monsters as well as even the End Dragon, a certain type of final monster you can fight.
details:
Social themes: the game features multiplayer allowign interactions on online servers where you can either play with strangers, or create worlds that are private to just you and your friends. You can start a life, buildign houses, gathering resources, trading with villagers, every part that you can do in your own freedom, can be done with another person or several people, making it fantastic for a a long term stay that allows multiple people to play at the same time and interact with each other or even for one person to constantly play on their own world for years on end. 
Calm: Minecraft is incredibly calming as it gives the palyer complete freedom in what they want to do, coupled with its simple, block like structures in art style as well as extremely relaxing music, Minecraft creates possibly the most calming experience you can find in gaming. 
Stress: Minecraft can help with stress as it is a very low stress game. There are certain features such as peaceful mode as well as keep inventory (where upon the players death, you do not lose any of the materials you are holding and therefore will not lose any progress from that) the game gives a lot of freedom in that aspect and that freedom is extremely relaxing
Pacing: Once again stated, the game is completely taken upon your own pace. There is no rush to building a home, mining for resources, or even an absolute necessity. There is no complete linear story progression or requirements so it is take at the player's pace

name: Nintendo Switch Sports
avatarGender: Human - able to design characters based on desire
playersOnline: Can be played online, but specifically meant for up to 4 people to play together in person
potentialConcerns: Can potentially get some amount of violence in the sense that since it is a party game - any game such as twister for example could upset some people when losing or if some players are being unfair.
summary: Nintendo Switch Sports puts some of the most popular sports such as basketball, volleyball, bowling, swordfighing (for fun), tennis and comprises them into party style games that are all extremely simple usually only comprising of about 2 -3 distinct movements that palyers make wiht their controllers. It is a very competitive game that kids are able to compete in as well as able to get exercise in and active fun throughout the several game modes.
details:
Social and teamwork: with any party game, this game is particularly social in the fact that teams are created and meant for people to compete against one another. - This lets for kids to really get involved as well as absorbed in the individual sports. 
Unique (sports anxiety?) I think this could be potentiallly a weird point, but several kids are scared of competing in real life sports as the competition as well as physcial aspect can create anxiety in lots of kids as well as some parents potentially barring it from children as a whole out of concern for safety. Nintendo Switch Sports allows for kids to feel stilll connected to modern sports without the real life aspect of it, and allows for them to not only feel safe in that aspect, but also can act as a bridge to connect kids towards those sports in making it seem easier to approach for children once they are familiar with the virtual aspect of it. 
Exercise: similar to just dance, it is an active style game that requires rapid physcial movement which allows for children to maintain levels of physcial activity.

name: Overcooked 1/2
avatarGender: Several! Either animals, humans, anything that the game has you can be as an avatar. Each avatar is a little miniscule version of either animals or people but they are quite adorable
playersOnline: Can be played solo, but highly recommend it for playing with friends or up to 4 people in a session
potentialConcerns: Could cause for potential harm in friendships due to the fact that the game can be tense and might have some good faith yelling (all in the fun though)
summary: Overcooked is a fast paced game that features solo as well as multiplayer with friends. Work together in intense sessions in where tasks such as cutting produce, cooking, cleaning dishes, and servig dishes have to be split and communicated between the players.
details:
Fast Paced: the game is incredibly fast paced, with time limits on tasks and food that is meant to be cooked and served, which helps with problems of boredom
Social interaction - definitely a very important part of the game. It allows for moderators who are spectating the game to see whether or not children/ the players could be potentially violent or aggressive when put in tense situations (so can be used to monitor behaviour effectively, without putting in a specific test) 
Stress: Can help with stress as the fast pace action in racing allows for players to have rapid dopamine from competing and potentiall let out a lot of pent up anxiety and stress.
Connection/teamwork: This is probably one of the few multiplayer games that really can deepen friendships in the traditional way in which difficuly trials strengthen bonds. There will be a lot of screaming and yelling, but all out of good fun, and this really allows for teamwork to be emphasized in a pretty simple manner. Teamwork is absolutely essential to performing well in this game, and without it, there is no way to really succeed, so it really is one of the best ways for teamwork to be easily developed amongst kids without requiring excessive communication.

name: Persona (the series - 3,4,5)
potentialConcerns: themes focused can be potentially too deep or too focused such as death or murder. Some images could be considered disturbing as well. As well as language.
summary: In each of the mainline persona games, you play as a character who has just arrived in a new school and city new to everything. There is essentially a alternate worlld in each game (the shadow world for example) that houses creepy spirits and monsters that seem to mindlessly attack or destroy any person. The protagonist and their team all find ways to activate their inner "Personas" that embody their truth and reality for their respective characters. The shadows as well as personas are all reflections on a persons inner soul and their "true thoughts" about how they both view themselves and the world. You and your team either are saving the world or solving a murder mystery. The game revolves on how the protagonist is able to use multiple of these personas and access different parts of themselves. Not only that but it features a "time based system" in which each  decision matters deeply for the player. Whether they want to go to dungeons or focus on spending time with their friends to strengthen their bonds. Persona follows its calender system to show that every decision matters
details:
Sadness, Grief, Loss: The persona games all do a really good job at highlighting loss and death, and how to recover. In each game there is usually a character that the player can interact with that teaches them the importance of acceptance and moving  on as well as embraces the time that can be still spent with them in the games story
Social connections /bonds: Within the game, it highly emphasizes that the conections created by the player with the surrounding cast is extremely important. Whether that be through unlocking special abilities or receiving benefits throughout the game. The player is able to learn more about a person the more time is spent with them, finding out their problems and sorrows as well as being able to help them wiht their issues. Persona gives a good depth to the character that allows for players to feel as if they are creating a more human bond than mosat games allow for
Decision making: Persona offers several endings in each game - all relating on the players choices with characters or villains throughout the story  as well as how they develop their relationships with their friends too. The time aspect of the game makes it show how important each diecision you make in your daily life is as well as just how you approach life
Morality: the persona villians are never explicitly evil. Depending on the game the villian usually acts out as "villanous" but for desire to do good in their own sense. It teaches an early sense of morality and how the world is not a true black and white and allows players to obtain very good life lessons in how they view people and how they can udnerstand people as well seeing teh characters playing out and understanding the intricacies of human minds

name: Portal 1&2
potentialConcerns: it can be scary with the narrator kind of, as well as sarcastic humor making fun of the players mistake but that’s about it
summary: You play as one, or two robots with another player in which you spawn into a sort of sandbox sort of world, where you are trapped, and used as a test dummy to solve puzzles to escape while being taunted by the overarching AI the whole time. Using your "portal gun" the player must solve physics based puzzles and set up their portals correctly throughout the rooms to correctly position bridges, keys, as well as the palyer to reach the end each time.
details:
Puzzles: Portal is one of the best "smart" games that can be given to players. It really requires players to think hard and wrack their brains around how to use their portal gun as well as movement and physics to solve puzzles. It teaches players understandings on momentum, movement, and to an extent, Physics!
Critical thinking and patience: you can NOT play the game without patience. The only way to pass the game is with taking lots of time, and trust with your own self - there is not really a rush concept through out the game but rather takes a lot of time to solve. It reallypays off when players are able to find little tricks and ways to eventually get to teh end and feels like a good reward.
TEAMWORK: the 2 player mode is significantly harder and requires a LOT of communication with players to be able to play through. It is great for teaching trust and buiild bonds through the difficulty of the game as well.

name: Stardew valley
playersOnline: Solo, or cooperative with up to 4 people.  online multiplayer (though most commonly played solo)
potentialConcerns: Can be scary with combat of some monsters such as bats, ghost, skeletons that do not have to be commonly interacted with at all.
summary: Stardew Valley starts off with the players character leaving their intense, and mentally crushing corporate job to inherit their grandparents farm. In the new town the player moves to, they can cultiate their farm to develop more land, farming, building, exploring and finding out several hidden things spread trhoughout the town, Not only that, players can interact and cultivate relationships with the fellow villagers and even marry or create several friendships all based on the players desires. It essentially is an "infinte game" similar to minecraft as there is not a definitive end to the game. This just lets the player practically do anything they work for
details:
Escapism: I think this is one of the games that are realistic enough despite being a 2D, pixel game, in the sense that players and people feel very real in the game and very organic. As well as the iontroduction taking players away from the PAINDUL REALITY they previously were in, It acts as a real safe haven for kids as well as adults to escape into. The farming and money system makes for a great way to understand economy as well as just a completely isolated ecosystem that is free for the player to be personally involved with to even the point where people can vote you as mayor. Stardew valley acts as the ultimate safety net and escape from reality that some  kids really need when dealing with Grief as well as sadness.
Social (solo)): similar to Animal crossing, players are able to interact with villagers, however stardew valley expands that much more and allows for relationships and friendships to be created more deeply. This helps extremely with social anxiety as it really encapsulates several aspects of creating friendships and interactions that real world people create due to how creative and interactive the Non Playable Characters (NPCs) (which are just in game people that you can interact with) are

name: Super Mario Odyssey
avatarGender: Male as well as a hat!
potentialConcerns: in my personal opinion I do not believe there is really any potential concerns with this game
summary: (expand) Mario, as all mario games go, finds princess peach being kidnapped, and with the help of a ghost who inhabits Mario's signature hat, he goes on a quest to save the galaxy from Bowser's tyyrany as well as to save Princess peach. Cappy, the ghost who inhabits mario's hat has the special ability to allow Mario to inhabit different creatures as well as objects allows him to become thigns such as Frogs, Dinosaurs, electricity, and several other things across the world that the player can find.
details:
Calm and pacing: These two coincide in Mario as Mario Odyssey is one of the largest free, open world games that features a ton of minigames and stories that allows players to pour in hundreds of hours. This game really takes it to a different level when allowing players to approach the game at their own speed and without making things overtly complex. Not only that, the action is not hard enough to throw players off from the difficulty, but the puzzles and story allows for players to really relax and enjoy their time in a positive game experience.
Kindness: The overall storyline of the game comprises a typical hero's story, in which an initial loss is suffered, and then the main character perseveres and eventually beats the bad guy and saves the princess, however, in Mario  Odyssey, the actions that mario takes around the world in helping support the people really makes a difference in the worlds he visits. Players can find cleaner cities, people thanking Mario, and overall really positive energy that is spread throughout the game as mario continues to do good deeds which allows that to be transmitted into children with a very straightforward way. The world becomes brighter and more cheery with each action that mario takes to save the galaxy that he encounters

name: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
genre: Role playing Game (RPG), Open world, action-adventure, puzzle
potentialConcerns: Potentially dark themes, though not crude at all. Suggested dark themes can include potential death, though not explicitally stated. A bit more of a mature game, with more
summary: Link, the main character wakes up with no memories after a 100 year healing/rest after having fallen in the final battle against a great monster. He wakes up in a world now unknown to him, as his faint memories call back to him to save his world.  You travel around the world known as "Hyrule" in which you explore one of the most artistically beautiful worlds in gaming. Interact with people as well as fight to free divine beasts that will help in the final battle against the demon lord Ganon.                                     Create bonds with people of different races such as the fish people known as Zora or the rock people Gorons as link travels the world to find both his memories as well as abilites and weapons to help him on his journey
details: One of the largest open world games that allows for player freedom. There is no explicit requirements after the tutorial of the game, so players are given infinite freedom in how they want to approach the wide world as well as are able to do whatever they want within the open world.                           BOTW can help with sadness, as the main character is someone who has gone through something extremly tragic and jarring, so having that sense of relatability within the protagonist creates a good connection earlyy on in the game as well as overall the sense of loss in the game with flashbacks of memories allows for players to revisit the joys of the past as well as understand the concepts of loss.                It can specifically help with anxiety because the game is extremely large. there are so mayn things that the game allows you to do yet not in a overwhelming way. Whether or not the player wants to confront puzzles using their mind rather than active combat, the ease and overall extremely beautiful game allows for a sense of ease despite the stress that can come from dungeons or bosses.                Specifically good for long term care as the game is a very large game that can take anybody from tens of hour to hundreds of hours to finish or to just continue exploring and enjoying the world. definitely a 10/10 for long term care

name: Undertale
potentialConcerns: some monsters can be scary - although the hardest part should just be that the game is kind of hard
summary: Undertale follows the story of a kid who falls down a hole and enters a different world under the earth that is completely different from anything on earth. It is where "monsters" reside. However, the monsters are not evil. They are just a race who have been sealed due to history. The story is focused on Frisk, the kid, and how they approach the world around them on their way to escape and make it back to the human world
details:
Consequnce: even more so than any other game, undertale highlights the consequences of the players actions. Not in just how there are different endings depending on whether the player is a kind pacifist, or a violent attacker, the way players either spare or even interact with characters changes the story severely in each part. From dialogue of monsters fearing you, or people even being nice or hostile towards you depending on your previous actions, the games difficulty as well asoverall gameplay all center on how the player approaches it. 
Mercy, kindness, determination:  One of undertale's main "routes" that you can play the game through is the mercy route, in which the player does not attack or defeat a single monster throughout the entire game. This is really important because it really spreads throughout the environment of the game. Characters show much mroe virtue love towards the player when  they exhibit kindness throughout the game, and through unique interactions with the bosses trhoughout the game, it really shows how kindess can allow even those hostile to you to begin reciprocating it back. The overall game tries to breakdown the player to become evil and despicable, but throughout each trial if the player repeatedly chooses kindness, the greateness of the game really shows through. The music is also reallllly good in this game
